commit.message:
Fix Social Auditor HTML error: eliminate SDK require() causing esbuild crash

Root cause: module-level `require('@google/generative-ai')` at the top of
social-media-auditor.js was causing esbuild bundling to fail silently.
When the function was invoked, Node crashed before the handler ran and
Netlify returned an HTML error page instead of JSON — producing the
"Unexpected token '<', <!DOCTYPE..." parse error in the frontend.

Fix: removed the SDK require entirely. Converted score-posts mode from
using GoogleGenerativeAI SDK to the same direct REST fetch() approach
already used by audit and deep-intake modes. No SDK imports anywhere
in the file — all three modes call the Gemini REST API directly.

Pattern to remember: never put require() at module level in Netlify
functions if there's any risk of bundling failure. Use REST APIs
directly (fetch) instead of SDKs when possible.

commit.message:
Release v4.6.3: Feature-by-Feature Logic Audit — Six Verified Bugs Fixed

- Bug 1: Post-sigmoid boost clipping creates ceiling effect — switched goal/entity boosts from additive to multiplicative to preserve relative ranking at top of results
- Bug 2: Score breakdown formatter omits context & priming components — added formatting for context(X) and priming(X) to detailed output
- Bug 3: NLI neutral fallthrough in conflict detection — NLI results with neutral label and low contradiction now correctly skip conflict reporting
- Bug 4: Unicode misclassification in token estimation — only count ASCII punctuation as non-alpha, treating non-ASCII (accented, CJK, emoji) as letters
- Bug 5: Browse mode hardcoded to insights weights — now averages effective weights across all 4 active categories
- Bug 6: Explicit feedback accumulation unbounded — added EXPLICIT_FEEDBACK_COMPONENT_CAP=10.0 to clamp per-component sums

All 2196 tests passing. Updated 2 existing tests to reflect corrected NLI neutral behavior. Added 12 new test cases for fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(executor): scheduleReconnect now triggers when tasks are queued but not executing

Previously scheduleReconnect() only checked this.executing.size > 0,
which meant tasks stuck in the queue (not yet executing) when the gateway
disconnected would never trigger a reconnect and remain stuck forever.

Also adds comprehensive test coverage for the agent module:
- gateway.test.ts (20 cases): device identity, Ed25519 signing, v3 handshake, token storage
- router-extended.test.ts (26 cases): propose, query, inbox management, hot-swap, message routing
- executor-extended.test.ts (7 cases): chat.update, content blocks, gateway error responses
- engine-extended.test.ts (23 cases): glob patterns, rate limiting, deepMerge, trust boundaries
- services-extended.test.ts (17 cases): tool conversion, tag inference, periodic refresh, multi-group

Total: 546 tests across 35 test files.

Fix UDP server, routing UX, voice grid nav, and voice editing

- UDP: replace async tokio server with blocking std::net::UdpSocket —
  eliminates silent panics from tokio runtime; errors now print to stderr
- main.rs: force F32 audio stream to avoid panic on unsupported formats
  (I32/U32 etc); spawn UDP thread before CPAL init
- Routing panel: ←→ now directly adjusts the selected cell's send level;
  Tab/Shift-Tab cycle through groups; Enter toggles 0%/100%; status bar
  shows a large value bar so changes are obvious
- Voice grid: arrow keys now traverse the 2×8 visual grid in row/column
  order (left/right/up/down move visually, not by index)
- Voice panel: added real editing — Enter enters edit mode; Tab cycles
  Oscillator → Envelope → Sends sections; ←→ adjusts values in each
  section; ↑↓ selects param within section; Esc returns to grid nav;
  'o' quick-cycles oscillator type from grid mode
- SynthState/AudioEngine: ADSR envelope params now included in state
  snapshot so TUI can display and edit live values

commit.message:
fix: 5 post-audit corrections — continuity, timing, reranker, concierge, proximity

- Affirmative session continuity: write accepted geo params back to
  session["accumulated_params"] before _run_search so next typed turn
  inherits the broadened city/province instead of stale prior location

- Advisor record timing: split _handle_zero_results into
  _diagnose_zero_results (records "advisor" step) + _show_zero_results
  (displays message). Diagnosis now runs BEFORE render_trace() so the
  advisor step is visible in the debug panel for the current turn.
  All three zero-results call sites updated.

- Reranker thinking preamble: replace fragile fence-stripping with
  re.search(r'\{.*\}', raw, re.DOTALL) matching intent_parser pattern.
  Handles Gemini thinking text prefix that caused JSON parse failures.

- CLARIFY_LOOP concierge: generate and speak concierge response when
  results exist (was silent). Concierge message captured in output record.

- Advisor proximity: pass lat/lon from merged_params to diagnose() so
  ST_Distance_Sphere ordering activates for proximity searches instead
  of always defaulting to program_count DESC.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: csc
repository.description: Camp Search Conceriege

commit.message:
fix: audit fixes — security, bugs, architecture, performance

Security:
- CORS AllowedOrigins reads from ALLOWED_ORIGIN env var (hardcoded localhost removed)
- Email format validation on registration
- Refresh endpoint no longer leaks internal error details
- Google OAuth upsert only links accounts when google_id is NULL
- JWT parser uses WithExpirationRequired()
- Mailer no-op log no longer prints invite token
- Frontend API base URL reads from VITE_API_BASE_URL env var

Bugs:
- AcceptInviteAtomic re-checks expiry atomically inside the tx (TOCTOU fix)
- AcceptInviteAtomic catches unique constraint violation → ErrAlreadyMember
- AcceptInvitePage useRef guard prevents double-POST in React StrictMode
- playbookStore fetchPlays/fetchPlay use Promise.all + clear stale state
- PlaybookPage/PlayEditorPage hydrate currentTeam on direct URL navigation

Architecture:
- Mailer depends on Sender interface, not concrete *Mailer
- Shared requireMember/requireCoach helpers in usecase/helpers.go
- Single writeUsecaseError replaces four duplicated handler error mappers
- Role/status magic strings replaced with constants in models/constants.go
- Sentinel errors: ErrInvalidCredentials, ErrNameRequired, ErrAlreadyMember
- Removed dead code: AddMember, RefreshTokenExpiresAt, UpdateInviteStatus

Performance:
- DB connection pool: MaxOpenConns(25), MaxIdleConns(10), ConnMaxLifetime(5m)
- Migration 000006: indexes on teams.coach_id + 6 other columns
- Separate isTeamLoading/isMembersLoading flags in teamStore

Code quality:
- Input uses useId() for stable label association
- PlayEditorPage uses crypto.randomUUID() for element IDs
- Error states added for team/playbook delete failures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(notifications): replace bespoke form with TwoStepNotificationsCTA (#5623)

The /[username]/notifications page had a completely separate hand-rolled
subscribe form (raw HTML button, inline styles, its own mutation logic)
that looked nothing like the rest of the product. Replace it with the
canonical TwoStepNotificationsCTA component already used on profile
pages — gets email/SMS toggle, OTP verification, animated two-step
flow, and design system styling for free. Page becomes a thin server
component that fetches the artist and renders the shared UI.

openInputFile, openNodeFile, runProcess),

everything together

fix: inject correct record count into trailer line for test runs

When record_count_check is enabled on a validate step, the runner reads
the trailer line of the FIXED-format input file to get the expected count.
In test mode the trailer line was written with all-blank values, causing
a downstream int("") ValueError after the NameError was fixed.

Changes:
- _write_fixed_input: accept optional trailer_data dict to override
  specific trailer field values when writing the test file
- _write_input_file: forward trailer_data to _write_fixed_input
- _prepare_run: pre-scan validate steps for record_count_check config,
  then pass the actual row count (under the configured trailer field name
  in hyphen/underscore variants) so the trailer line carries a valid
  integer and the count check passes in test mode

commit.message:
fix(infra): add HeadBucket permission to task role (#512)

* fix(infra): add S3 pack permissions to dashboard task role

The ECS task definition uses 'enkai-dashboard-task-dev' (legacy manual role)
but the IAM stack manages 'enkai-qualify-dev-task'. The grantReadWrite in CDK
was granting to a role that's never used. This adds an IAM::Policy resource
that attaches S3 pack bucket access to the actual dashboard task role.

Root cause: pack generation failing with "S3 bucket not found or inaccessible"
despite the bucket existing — the container simply lacked S3 permissions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(infra): revert failed IAM policy, add HeadBucket to task role

Remove the DashboardPackAccessPolicy that failed due to pipeline role
permissions. Instead, add s3:HeadBucket and s3:GetBucketLocation to the
managed TaskRole. Task definition updated to revision 40 to use this role.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: block AI crawlers + cap pagination to stop 1244GB egress spike

GPTBot was crawling ?page=1 through ?page=532+ on every city/state page,
bypassing ISR cache and triggering 728K+ SELECT * queries against Supabase.

- Block GPTBot, ClaudeBot, ChatGPT-User, meta-externalagent, Go-http-client
  in middleware (enforced at edge, not advisory like robots.txt)
- Cap ?page= param at 100 in middleware (return 404 for absurd values)
- Replace SELECT * existence checks with COUNT(*) queries
  (hasClinicsByState/hasClinicsByCity instead of getClinicsByState/City)
- Disallow AI bots in robots.ts (were previously allowed: allow ["/"])
- Fix unused import in find-and-clean-broadcast script
